title: Mario Robotti

infobox.value: (25 November 1882 – 1955) was a general in the [Royal Italian Army](/wiki/Royal_Italian_Army "Royal Italian Army") who commanded the [XI Corps](/wiki/XI_Corps_(Italy) "XI Corps (Italy)") during the [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II") [Axis](/wiki/Axis_powers "Axis powers") [invasion of Yugoslavia](/wiki/Invasion_of_Yugoslavia "Invasion of Yugoslavia") in April 1941.[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTENafziger19971–7-1)

Morgan, Philip (2008-06-11). [*The Fall of Mussolini: Italy, the Italians, and the Second World War*](https://books.google.com/books?id=-UttMMRXi9AC). OUP Oxford. From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/bd/Taddeo_Orlando_and_Mario_Robotti.jpg/250px-Taddeo_Orlando_and_Mario_Robotti.jpg)](/wiki/File:Taddeo_Orlando_and_Mario_Robotti.jpg)

Robotti (right) talking with [Taddeo Orlando](/wiki/Taddeo_Orlando "Taddeo Orlando")

**Mario Robotti** (25 November 1882 – 1955) was a general in the [Royal Italian Army](/wiki/Royal_Italian_Army "Royal Italian Army") who commanded the [XI Corps](/wiki/XI_Corps_(Italy) "XI Corps (Italy)") during the [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II") [Axis](/wiki/Axis_powers "Axis powers") [invasion of Yugoslavia](/wiki/Invasion_of_Yugoslavia "Invasion of Yugoslavia") in April 1941.[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTENafziger19971–7-1)

He then became military commander in the [Province of Ljubljana](/wiki/Province_of_Ljubljana "Province of Ljubljana"), the Italian-annexed zone of [Slovenia](/wiki/Slovenia "Slovenia"). In February 1943, he succeeded general [Mario Roatta](/wiki/Mario_Roatta "Mario Roatta") as commander of the Italian [Second Army](/wiki/Second_Army_(Italy) "Second Army (Italy)") in occupied Yugoslavia. General Robotti while commanding Italian troops in Axis occupied Yugoslavia in anti-[partisan](/wiki/Yugoslav_Partisans "Yugoslav Partisans") operations, was noted for his brutality, even once complaining to his officers that "you are not slaughtering enough people." During the winter of 1942–1943, Robotti personally chose 10 local dignitaries from the Slovene capital, [Ljubljana](/wiki/Ljubljana "Ljubljana"), and had them imprisoned in the city and then stated that they would be executed if the partisans chose to make an attack on the city.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

At the time of the [Armistice of Cassibile](/wiki/Armistice_of_Cassibile "Armistice of Cassibile") in September 1943, he escaped capture by the Germans and retired to a private life in Rapallo, where he lived with his family.
